summary: Iteration 2 — CoinDCX/INR migration + safety + backtest + alerts. Backend: 18/18 pytest pass against public preview (root exchange=coindcx/INR, INR tickers, BTCINR klines, portfolio reset 3000 with 100-20000 validation, manual BUY/SELL with quantity_inr, AI signal Claude on BTCINR, backtest endpoint runs and returns equity_curve+trades, bot start/stop with safety config trailing+agree+dayloss, alerts list+clear). Frontend: After fixing a build-blocking import error in LiveSignals.jsx (fmtUsd→fmtInr), full dashboard renders. Verified tabs (LIVE TRADING / BACKTEST), INR ticker strip with ₹ prices, BTC/INR chart, Portfolio inline reset form + confirm, BotControl trailing-stop-toggle / agree-input / dayloss-input, start-bot-btn → stop-bot-btn flow, AlertsPanel + clear-alerts-btn, BacktestPanel run-backtest-btn produces all 6 KPIs (Return +0.30%, WinRate 0.0%, Trades 1, MaxDD 0.1%, Final ₹3,009, P&L -₹2) and equity curve renders.

minor.issue: backtest.py line 97 filters closed = [t for t in trades if t['type'] in ('STOP_LOSS','TAKE_PROFIT','END_CLOSE')] — but strategy-signal exits are recorded with type='CLOSE' (line 77). Result: total_trades, wins, losses, win_rate_pct, total_pnl, profit_factor all ignore strategy SELL closures. Reproduced with payload {symbol:'BTCINR',interval:'1h',limit:500,initial_balance:3000,min_confidence:0.55,min_strategies_agree:1}: returned total_trades=0 while trades[] contained 22 OPEN/CLOSE pairs with PnL. Fix: include 'CLOSE' in the filter on line 97.

ui_bugs.issue: Quick amount buttons use data-testid='quick-100/500/1000/2000' but spec requested 'quick-amount-100/...'. Functional but breaks test selectors per review_request.

issues: Recharts width(-1)/height(-1) console warning on first paint; cosmetic, chart renders correctly afterwards. Same as iteration 1.

action_items: Fix /app/backend/backtest.py line 97: include 'CLOSE' in closed-trade filter so strategy SELL exits are counted in total_trades / win_rate_pct / total_pnl / profit_factor.
Optional: rename ManualTradePanel quick-amount data-testids from 'quick-{v}' to 'quick-amount-{v}' per review request.
Optional: silence recharts width/height warning on PriceChart initial mount (parent container has 0 height before layout settles).
critical_code_review_comments: backtest.py: Hard-coded total_trades filter is the root cause of misreported metrics — see backend minor issue.
server.py: portfolio_reset wipes trades+signals+alerts every reset which is fine for paper-only but destructive; consider keeping a history collection.
server.py: /api/signals/{symbol} action-resolution ternary still complex (line 134); extract helper.

Frontend/lib/api.js — only fmtInr/fmtNum/fmtPct/shortSym are exported; LiveSignals.jsx had stale fmtUsd import that crashed the entire app at compile time. Migration cleanup was incomplete — recommend a global grep for legacy symbols (USD, USDT, fmtUsd, quantity_usd) before next iteration.
BotControl exposes new safety fields with proper data-testid; good.

success_rate.frontend: ≈95% (all critical flows OK; one testid name mismatch)

seed_data_creation: Portfolio reset to ₹3000 INR multiple times during tests; alerts cleared at end; ETHINR ₹300 BUY+SELL roundtrip executed; bot start/stop cycle executed; one backtest run with 500 candles on BTCINR 1h.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (18 tests, ~14s). Uses python-dotenv to read /app/frontend/.env for REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Trade docs use side ('BUY'/'SELL') and type ('MANUAL','BOT','CLOSE','STOP_LOSS','TAKE_PROFIT','OPEN','END_CLOSE'). Backtest type='CLOSE' is currently uncounted in metrics — see action item #1. AI signal endpoint takes 25-40s. Backtest endpoint takes 10-20s. After tests run, alerts collection is empty (last test clears it).
rca of the issue: Backtest metric undercount RCA: /app/backend/backtest.py line 56-81 emits two exit types — line 49 emits 'STOP_LOSS'/'TAKE_PROFIT' (when price hits SL/TP), line 77 emits 'CLOSE' (when aggregate strategy signals SELL with confidence+agreement). Line 97 then computes closed = [t for t in trades if t['type'] in ('STOP_LOSS','TAKE_PROFIT','END_CLOSE')] — 'CLOSE' is excluded. Therefore any backtest where strategy SELL exits dominate (most realistic scenarios with low confidence threshold) reports total_trades=0, win_rate_pct=0, total_pnl=0, profit_factor=0 even when the trades[] tape clearly shows winning roundtrips. Mitigation: add 'CLOSE' to the tuple on line 97. The existing wins/losses arithmetic on lines 98-103 will then work correctly because each CLOSE record already carries pnl. No other code path needs to change.
